title: paspalum axillare
intro: Paspalum axillare, also known as buffalo grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Africa and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Paspalum axillare has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The herbal preparations of Paspalum axillare include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat specific ailments and promote overall well-being.
